blank_semantics: Blank means absent, not populated, or not audited. It must not be interpreted as No, None known, or false.

canonical_merge.stable_identity: The target/base record_id remains the canonical identity.
canonical_merge.overlay_rule: Only nonblank correction values overlay the target.
canonical_merge.blank_rule: Blank correction fields never erase populated target values.
canonical_merge.ordering_rule: Corrections are order-independent; collect all rows before resolution.
canonical_merge.multiple_corrections: Apply by numeric batch, then revision, then file-defined deterministic tie-breaker. Preserve all applied correction IDs.

Schema identifier. Future files use EQL-LOOT-1.0.
Stable canonical identity after normalization; normally the original record_id.
Optional parent for encounter, metadata, or grouped records.
Whether the row is shown in ordinary results before merging/filtering.
Whether the row may appear in search or research mode.
Normalized faction risk. Blank means not audited.
Guard, merchant, citizen, quest NPC, guild NPC, or other protected role.
Broad family, such as Goblin or Undead.
Named shared table or research grouping; never implies universal drops.
Faction associated with the family or population.
Semicolon-delimited zones where this exact family definition applies.
Camp, room, building, island, or encounter area.
Coordinates as published; preserve source convention.
Named encounter, which may differ from source_npc.
Whether the encounter has scripted behavior.
Whether the spawn is guaranteed under stated conditions.
Need/greed, assigned, corpse, or other roll context if relevant.
Chance that the relevant pool is selected.
Chance that this item is selected from the pool.
Door, lockpick, key, or switch requirements.
Whether source is treated as a raid target.
Groups related content without merging it.
Rare escape hatch. Common/filterable data must use normal fields.
